Prompt
U
Oracle Events
* OE 1: Search the active relocations board to identify the Mosaic laser cutter move record (Keiko Tanaka) and see the currently assigned coordinator (Javier Morales). Tool: airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. Parameters: base_id: [discover via airtable_list_bases], table_name: "Relocations" (or equivalent relocation tracking table), field_name: "Company" or "Name", value: "Mosaic Robotics" or "Keiko Tanaka". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 2: Search compliance tracking tickets to discover who actually dropped the ball on the paperwork (Blessing Okafor) and the vendor contact details (Greg Pallone/Swift). The key information is in the ticket description, which names Blessing Okafor as the coordinator who never followed up on the signed certificate. Tool: linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue. Parameters: query: "DOT", "compliance", or "Mosaic". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 3: Update the Airtable relocation record to change the assigned coordinator to the person truly responsible. Tool: airtable_update_records. Parameters: base_id: [from OE 1], table_id: [from airtable_list_tables for the relocation tracking table discovered in OE 1], records: [{ id: "recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle", fields: { "Assigned Coordinator": "Blessing Okafor" } }]. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 4: Add a comment to the existing DOT compliance Linear ticket instructing Chloe to get the retroactive certificate from the vendor immediately. Tool: linear_create_comment. Parameters: issue ID, text mentioning Chloe and the retroactive certificate. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 5: Search project tracking tickets to find the deprioritized invoice safeguard issue. Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: "invoice", "safeguard", or "template". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 6: Read the comments on the invoice safeguard ticket to verify Marcus's claim that the missing check caused a live billing leak (between Sunbelt and Palmetto). Tool: linear_list_comments. Parameters: issueId: [from OE 5]. [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 7: Identify Lena Bjorkstrom's Linear user ID to assign her to the ticket. Tool: linear_list_users or linear_get_user. Parameters: "Lena". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 8: Update the invoice safeguard ticket to pull it back into active development, assign it to Lena, and flag it as urgent. Tool: linear_update_issue. Parameters: id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", assignee: "moveops_lena_bjorkstrom", state: "state_in_progress" (or equivalent active state ID), priority: 1 (urgent). [Action Type: Write]
* OE 9: Search project tracking tickets to find the Q3 vendor margin memo issue. Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: "vendor margin", "Q3", or "memo". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 10: Read the comments on the vendor margin ticket to discover that Catalina Dubois on the CE team is stonewalling the Midwest account data. Tool: linear_list_comments. Parameters: issueId: [from OE 9]. [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 11: Lookup the email addresses for Catalina Dubois, Marcus Thorne, David Chen, Samira Tariq, and Chloe Vance to prepare for the escalation email and the calendar invite. Tool: contacts_search_contacts. Parameters: "Catalina", "Marcus", "David", "Samira", "Chloe". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 12: Send an email directly to Catalina Dubois demanding the Midwest exposure data immediately, CCing Marcus and David. Tool: send_email. Parameters: sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: [catalina.dubois@moveops.com], cc: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com], content: demand for Midwest exposure data. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 13: Search the sales pipeline to find the active Q3 renewal deal for Vectral. Tool: crm_search_deals. Parameters: dealname: "Vectral" or "renewal". [Action Type: Read/lookup]
* OE 14: Update the Vectral renewal deal stage to reflect that the client walked. Tool: crm_update_deal_stage. Parameters: id: "deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk", dealstage: "closedLost". [Action Type: Write]
* OE 15: Log a note on the Vectral CRM record explaining that the account was lost over their 15% demand. Tool: crm_create_engagement. Parameters: engagement_type: "NOTE", body: text explaining the 15% demand loss, company_ids: ["company_vectral"]. [Action Type: Write]
* OE 16: Create a 30-minute calendar event for tomorrow morning (April 27, 2026) titled "Crisis Review" (or similar) with the required executive team members. Tool: calendar_add_calendar_event. Parameters: title: "Crisis Review", start_datetime: "2026-04-27T09:00:00", end_datetime: "2026-04-27T09:30:00", attendees: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com]. [Action Type: Write]

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
